records.title: Non-revenue water percentage
records.summary: Track the share of treated water lost to leakage, metering errors, and unauthorized consumption across district metered areas.
records.metric_definition: System input volume minus billed authorized consumption, divided by system input volume.
records.methodology_note: Use IWA water balance methodology with minimum night flow analysis for real-loss estimation.

provenance.citation: World Bank Water Supply and Sanitation

records.title: Drinking water quality compliance rate
records.summary: Measure the percentage of samples meeting microbiological and chemical standards at customer taps by zone.
records.metric_definition: Compliant samples divided by total samples tested per reporting period.

records.title: Environmental discharge compliance
records.summary: Maintain treated effluent quality within licence limits with real-time monitoring and automated alerting on exceedance risk.

records.title: Data-driven asset renewal prioritization
records.summary: Prioritize pipe and infrastructure renewal using condition data, failure history, consequence of failure, and remaining useful life models.

records.title: Climate resilience and water security
records.summary: Integrate climate projections, demand forecasting, and supply-side modelling to secure long-term water availability under drought and growth scenarios.

records.title: Smart network operations
records.summary: Deploy sensor networks, digital twins, and AI-driven pressure management to reduce leakage, energy costs, and supply interruptions.

records.title: Combine network sensor data with asset condition history for predictive intervention
records.summary: Prioritize use cases that join real-time pressure and flow sensor data with pipe age, material, soil type, and failure history to predict where bursts and leakage will occur, enabling targeted intervention before service disruption.

records.title: Produce water balance metrics aligned to IWA methodology for regulators
records.summary: Prefer use cases that output non-revenue water, real losses, and apparent losses structured to IWA water balance methodology so results can directly feed regulatory submissions and benchmark comparisons without manual reclassification.
